criteria.criterion: Required authority and proxy files were read
criteria.evidence: Read AGENTS.md, master spec, current state/checkpoint/decision/changelog files, AUTONOMOUS_EVAL_SOURCE_APPROVAL.md, scripts/codex_proxy.py, both codex_proxy schemas, and codex_proxy_synthetic evidence. Core file hashes are recorded above.

criteria.criterion: Requested versus actual model recording test
criteria.evidence: Process metadata records requested_model default, actual_model unknown, and actual_model_recording_status RECORDED_FROM_CHILD_OUTPUT for both child runs. Limitation: this verifies field capture, not an authoritative runtime model identity beyond the child output.

criteria.criterion: No project/customer data in codex exec prompts
criteria.evidence: Parsed final command arguments show synthetic-only prompts using project_id SYNTH-CJK-001, fixture-only roles, and fixed output labels. No real project/customer identifiers or source paths appear in the prompt strings.

docs/01_CURRENT_STATE.md and docs/SESSION_CHECKPOINT.md state real generation remains blocked with no generator-approved files or worksheets; this proxy audit does not override the source-approval gate.

criteria.criterion: Bundled Git clean-status check before writes
criteria.evidence: Immediately before creating the audit files, bundled git reported HEAD 5754abb4ad0054484f7c3609befe7333021d3fc3, empty `git status --short`, and empty `git diff --stat`.

limitations: The audit did not rerun `scripts/codex_proxy.py synthetic-suite` because that command writes into reports/codex_proxy_synthetic evidence paths outside the two authorized audit outputs.
The model-recording test captures the child-reported `actual_model` field. The recorded value is `unknown`; this is adequate for the existing synthetic gate but not authoritative model telemetry.
This status covers codex proxy synthetic readiness only. Real project generation remains blocked until source approval and sanitized-bundle verification pass.
